verses.latin_vulgate: Recordare, Domine, quid acciderit nobis ;  intuere et respice opprobrium nostrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְכֹ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ מֶֽה־הָ֣יָה לָ֔נוּ (הביט) [הַבִּ֖יטָה] וּרְאֵ֥ה אֶת־חֶרְפָּתֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Remember, O Lord, what is come upon us: consider, and behold our reproach.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæreditas nostra versa est ad alienos,  domus nostræ ad extraneos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַחֲלָתֵ֙נוּ֙ נֶֽהֶפְכָ֣ה לְזָרִ֔ים בָּתֵּ֖ינוּ לְנׇכְרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Our inheritance is turned to strangers, our houses to aliens.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pupilli facti sumus absque patre,  matres nostræ quasi viduæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְתוֹמִ֤ים הָיִ֙ינוּ֙ (אין) [וְאֵ֣ין] אָ֔ב אִמֹּתֵ֖ינוּ כְּאַלְמָנֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: We are orphans and fatherless, our mothers are as widows.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aquam nostram pecunia bibimus ;  ligna nostra pretio comparavimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵימֵ֙ינוּ֙ בְּכֶ֣סֶף שָׁתִ֔ינוּ עֵצֵ֖ינוּ בִּמְחִ֥יר יָבֹֽאוּ׃
verses.king_james: We have drunken our water for money; our wood is sold unto us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cervicibus nostris minabamur,  lassis non dabatur requies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֤ל צַוָּארֵ֙נוּ֙ נִרְדָּ֔פְנוּ יָגַ֖עְנוּ (לא) [וְלֹ֥א] הֽוּנַֽח־לָֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Our necks are under persecution: we labour, and have no rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ægypto dedimus manum et Assyriis,  ut saturaremur pane.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ נָתַ֣נּוּ יָ֔ד אַשּׁ֖וּר לִשְׂבֹּ֥עַֽ לָֽחֶם׃
verses.king_james: We have given the hand to the Egyptians, and to the Assyrians, to be satisfied with bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Patres nostri peccaverunt, et non sunt :  et nos iniquitates eorum portavimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲבֹתֵ֤ינוּ חָֽטְאוּ֙ (אינם) [וְאֵינָ֔ם] (אנחנו) [וַאֲנַ֖חְנוּ] עֲוֺנֹתֵיהֶ֥ם סָבָֽלְנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Our fathers have sinned, and are not; and we have borne their iniquities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servi dominati sunt nostri :  non fuit qui redimeret de manu eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲבָדִים֙ מָ֣שְׁלוּ בָ֔נוּ פֹּרֵ֖ק אֵ֥ין מִיָּדָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Servants have ruled over us: there is none that doth deliver us out of their hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: In animabus nostris afferebamus panem nobis,  a facie gladii in deserto.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנַפְשֵׁ֙נוּ֙ נָבִ֣יא לַחְמֵ֔נוּ מִפְּנֵ֖י חֶ֥רֶב הַמִּדְבָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: We gat our bread with the peril of our lives because of the sword of the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pellis nostra quasi clibanus exusta est,  a facie tempestatum famis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עוֹרֵ֙נוּ֙ כְּתַנּ֣וּר נִכְמָ֔רוּ מִפְּנֵ֖י זַלְעֲפ֥וֹת רָעָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Our skin was black like an oven because of the terrible famine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulieres in Sion humiliaverunt,  et virgines in civitatibus Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָשִׁים֙ בְּצִיּ֣וֹן עִנּ֔וּ בְּתֻלֹ֖ת בְּעָרֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: They ravished the women in Zion, and the maids in the cities of Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes manu suspensi sunt ;  facies senum non erubuerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׂרִים֙ בְּיָדָ֣ם נִתְל֔וּ פְּנֵ֥י זְקֵנִ֖ים לֹ֥א נֶהְדָּֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: Princes are hanged up by their hand: the faces of elders were not honoured.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adolescentibus impudice abusi sunt,  et pueri in ligno corruerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּחוּרִים֙ טְח֣וֹן נָשָׂ֔אוּ וּנְעָרִ֖ים בָּעֵ֥ץ כָּשָֽׁלוּ׃
verses.king_james: They took the young men to grind, and the children fell under the wood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Senes defecerunt de portis,  juvenes de choro psallentium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְקֵנִים֙ מִשַּׁ֣עַר שָׁבָ֔תוּ בַּחוּרִ֖ים מִנְּגִינָתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: The elders have ceased from the gate, the young men from their musick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Defecit gaudium cordis nostri ;  versus est in luctum chorus noster.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁבַת֙ מְשׂ֣וֹשׂ לִבֵּ֔נוּ נֶהְפַּ֥ךְ לְאֵ֖בֶל מְחֹלֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: The joy of our heart is ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cecidit corona capitis nostri :  væ nobis, quia peccavimus !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָֽפְלָה֙ עֲטֶ֣רֶת רֹאשֵׁ֔נוּ אֽוֹי־נָ֥א לָ֖נוּ כִּ֥י חָטָֽאנוּ׃
verses.king_james: The crown is fallen from our head: woe unto us, that we have sinned!

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea mœstum factum est cor nostrum ;  ideo contenebrati sunt oculi nostri,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־זֶ֗ה הָיָ֤ה דָוֶה֙ לִבֵּ֔נוּ עַל־אֵ֖לֶּה חָשְׁכ֥וּ עֵינֵֽינוּ׃
verses.king_james: For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes are dim.

verses.latin_vulgate: propter montem Sion quia disperiit ;  vulpes ambulaverunt in eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֤ל הַר־צִיּוֹן֙ שֶׁשָּׁמֵ֔ם שׁוּעָלִ֖ים הִלְּכוּ־בֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Because of the mountain of Zion, which is desolate, the foxes walk upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu autem, Domine, in æternum permanebis,  solium tuum in generationem et generationem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַתָּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ לְעוֹלָ֣ם תֵּשֵׁ֔ב כִּסְאֲךָ֖ לְד֥וֹר וָדֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: Thou, O Lord, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare in perpetuum oblivisceris nostri,  derelinques nos in longitudine dierum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֤מָּה לָנֶ֙צַח֙ תִּשְׁכָּחֵ֔נוּ תַּֽעַזְבֵ֖נוּ לְאֹ֥רֶךְ יָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, and forsake us so long time?

verses.latin_vulgate: Converte nos, Domine, ad te, et convertemur ;  innova dies nostros, sicut a principio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲשִׁיבֵ֨נוּ יְהֹוָ֤ה ׀ אֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ (ונשוב) [וְֽנָשׁ֔וּבָה] חַדֵּ֥שׁ יָמֵ֖ינוּ כְּקֶֽדֶם׃
verses.king_james: Turn thou us unto thee, O Lord, and we shall be turned; renew our days as of old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed projiciens repulisti nos :  iratus es contra nos vehementer.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֚י אִם־מָאֹ֣ס מְאַסְתָּ֔נוּ קָצַ֥פְתָּ עָלֵ֖ינוּ עַד־מְאֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.
